developer_response.body: Hey there! Sorry to hear the game isn't for you. If you do want to give it another shot, though, there are configurations for the stickiness you experience to make the locomotion more to your taste-

reviews.review_description: I’ve always hated gorilla tag and all its ripoffs for the controls, but this one was pretty tolerable. It feels really off though, as when walking, you go too fast and it’s really sensitive, but when trying to jump, you don’t get enough height, making a lot of the areas way too difficult. The wings are pretty useless. They halo you get to secret areas and save your life from a near miss, but it either doesn’t go I. The direction you’re facing, or doesn’t cause you to move at all. The limit of 3 flaps also really limits its use. Combat is okay, but doesn’t really require strategy, so it’s just swinging your arms until something happens. The game was a solid 4.5/5 until the game went on way too long and got soooo repetitive and as it ramps up the difficulty, you notice how janky the controls are to use. As for visuals, it’s pretty good until you notice how many spots just look unfinished. It’s a solid game, but it would be much better if the controls were smoother and the combat was more than just an annoying obstacle.

reviews.review_title: This game is such a hype!
reviews.review_description: This game offers a huge challenge for experienced vr players which is an amazing thing for a gtag movement game. the characters are unique and the story is great, the only problem i had with the game was the lack of replayability but that was since changed with a new update adding a wave defence like brawl mode which was a great addition to the game. The idea of freeing the other experiments that are all around the map for skins was also a very nice touch as you could explore the map much more and discover even more things. 10/10 game!

Outta Hand is a solid VR game with a live world, expressive characters, and challenging activities to conquer. The reason that I think this game deserves 4 stars is because there is little to no replay ability throughout all the activities. There is an endless mode, yes, but even that repeats after a few levels.
I really like this game it gives you a lot of free skins. I like it .five stars! and can you add hand tracking in multiplayer to your game, please?

This game is actually one of (if not) the best vr game EVER. No offense to the No More Rainbows fans and creators but the bosses battles are actually good. The No More Rainbows bosses feel the same. Same character and you're just chasing someone. But in this game theres great combat and puzzles in them which is what bosses should do. Not to mention that the game is highly replayable and the story doesn't affect way to much of the gameplay. AND not to mention (again) Theres an endless fighting and running gamemode that are just as fun as the story mode.

reviews.review_title: I think I might have ADHD
reviews.review_description:
Only past the first few levels, and I absolutely LOVE this game! Don’t know why some people say the controls are jank, yes it does take a lot of energy to propel yourself, but you can move with one hand too. And the hands are a bit close to the floor but i don’t mind. I like having to find the hidden chromosomes around the map. You can also wear the hats the broom robots wear which I find funny. 

High-energy fighting games aren’t usually my thing, but I was so amazed when I saw this game that I needed to buy it. I knew from the start this was gonna be a hidden gem. The fighting mechanics are comfortable, you don’t need to get ridiculously close to the robots to punch them but still pretty near, I appreciate not having to put my face in them. Definitely a game best played sitting down if you have the space.

This game gets my mark of silly little robot approval. 👍 If you are neurodivergent you should totally buy this game.
